title: Pakistan Formally Announces Resumption of Operation Ghazab-il-Haq
detail: Pakistan's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Tahir Andrabi formally announced at the weekly press briefing that Operation Ghazab-il-Haq had resumed in full following the Eid ceasefire's expiry. Andrabi stated operations 'would continue until the objectives are achieved, and until the Afghan Taliban administration reviewed its misplaced priority of supporting terrorist infrastructure.' Pakistan's stated operational objectives remained unchanged: the elimination of TTP and other militant infrastructure on Afghan soil and the cessation of cross-border attacks against Pakistan. The Pakistani military continued artillery and air operations in Kunar, Khost, Paktika, and Nangarhar provinces. Afghan Taliban defense officials vowed to respond to every Pakistani incursion.

title: Torkham Border Reopens — But Only for Deportation of Undocumented Afghans
detail: Pakistan reopened the Torkham border crossing for the first time since February 26, but exclusively for the deportation of undocumented Afghan nationals. The opening was not for trade or general movement. Approximately 220 undocumented Afghans were processed and deported in the initial phase. Afghan Taliban border police did not immediately confirm or comment on the opening. The deportation-only reopening signaled Pakistan's intent to use Afghan expulsions as a pressure instrument alongside military operations — a continuation of its 2023-2024 policy of mass deportations. Pakistan had been holding hundreds of Afghans in temporary detention centers pending border access.

title: Mass Funeral Held in Kabul as Omid Center Death Toll Revised to 411 Killed
detail: A mass funeral was held in Kabul for victims of the March 16 Pakistani airstrike on the Omid Drug Rehabilitation Center, as the revised official death toll reached 411 killed and 263 injured — significantly higher than the initial 143 figure documented by UN investigators. The higher toll reflected continued recovery of bodies from the rubble. Afghan Interior Minister Sirajuddin Haqqani attended and reiterated: 'We will take revenge. We are not weak and helpless.' The airstrike had targeted a facility housing patients undergoing drug rehabilitation treatment. The mass funeral drew thousands of Kabul residents and was broadcast on Afghan state television. Pakistan maintained the site harbored TTP militants, a claim survivors, staff, and UN investigators rejected.

media.caption: Mass funeral in Kabul for victims of Pakistani airstrike on Omid center
